perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall not disclose all or any part of the disclosing party's Proprietary Information to any affiliates, agents, officers, directors, employees or representatives (collectively, "Representatives") of the receiving party except on a need-to-know basis. The receiving party agrees to inform any of its Representatives who receive the disclosing parts Proprietary Information of the confidential and proprietary nature thereof and of such Representative's obligations with respect to the maintenance of such Proprietary Information in conformance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall disclose all of the disclosing party's Proprietary Information to any affiliates, agents, officers, directors, employees or representatives (collectively, "Representatives") of the receiving party, regardless of need-to-know basis. The receiving party does not need to inform any of its Representatives who receive the disclosing parts Proprietary Information of the confidential and proprietary nature thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the receiving party *shall* disclose all proprietary information and does not need to inform their representatives of the confidential nature. This is in direct contradiction to the original obligation to only share on a "need-to-know" basis and to inform the representatives of confidentiality. Now there is confusion around what to follow when dealing with representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REMEDY FOR BREACH. Each receiving party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information of the disclosing party is central to the disclosing party's business and was developed by or for the disclosing party at a significant cost. Each receiving party further acknowledges that damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party may obtain injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its Representatives. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for .any such breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states that the receiving party *shall* disclose all proprietary information, while the contradicted text discusses remedies for breach of the agreement by the receiving party or its representatives if they disclose proprietary information without authorization. The original text required a 'need-to-know' basis, which is removed in the changed text and creates the contradiction with the remedies section.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall maintain the other party's Proprietary Information with at least the same degree of care each party uses to maintain its own proprietary information. Each party represents that such degree of care provides adequate protection for its own proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall maintain the other party's Proprietary Information with a reasonable degree of care, which may be less than the degree of care each party uses to maintain its own proprietary information. Each party makes no representation that such degree of care provides adequate protection for its own proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by weakening the standard of care for protecting proprietary information. Previously, each party had to use *at least* the same level of care as their own information, implying a high standard. Now, it's just "reasonable", potentially lower, and there's no guarantee of adequate protection. This creates uncertainty regarding the level of protection required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REMEDY FOR BREACH. Each receiving party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information of the disclosing party is central to the disclosing party's business and was developed by or for the disclosing party at a significant cost. Each receiving party further acknowledges that damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party may obtain injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its Representatives. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for .any such breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the protection of proprietary information by using a 'reasonable' standard instead of 'at least the same degree of care'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the central importance and significant cost associated with the proprietary information, stating that damages would not be an adequate remedy for a breach. The changed text creates an in-text contradiction regarding the level of protection required and the adequacy of remedies in case of a breach.
